project.title: Crude extract of a novel actinomycete Streptomyces sp. PSRA5 isolated from Panax sokpayensis.
project.projectDescription: An actinomycete, Streptomyces sp. PSRA5 was isolated from Panax sokpayensis, a medicinal plant of the Sikkim Himalayas. It was found to have antimicrobial activity against multi-drug resistant Staphylococcus aureus. The crude extract of the actinomycete was extracted and analysed.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 1. 0.5 gm of actinomycetes strain PSRA5 was inoculated into 250 ml Erlenmeyer flasks containing 150 ml of CSPYME media and incubated for 25 days at 220 rpm at 28℃.  2. After incubation was complete, the aggregate of cells was separated from the broth using a sterile filter paper. The cells were dried and mass was measured as 5.25 gms.  3. The broth was then mixed with equal volumes of ETHYL ACETATE (150 ml) and extracted using a rotary evaporator. 4. The resulting extract was taken in an Eppendorf and air dried. The weight of the dried extract was found to be 1.325 gms. 5. 1 ml of ethyl acetate was again added to the Eppendorf resulting in a final concentration of 1.325gms/ml that was submitted to the LC-MS facility. 6. Sample (ethyl acetate extract) was vortexed well. 7. The dried sample was reconstituted in 110ul of 100% methanol. 8. 10ul of sample was dried under vacuum and to this 20ul each of reserpine and taurocholate-D8 was added. 9. The reconstituted sample was centrifuged at 14800rpm for 10mins at 4degrees and 10ul of sample supernatant was injected (in duplicate) for analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Metabolite Lists with filtered data with available annotation from mzCloud. (Analysis on Compound Discoverer 3.2) and ChemSpider search results from KEGG, HMDB, MassBank, E.coli Metabolome Database, LipidMaps.

Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: LC-MS analysis of the ethyl acetate extract of S. panacea PSRA5T culture supernatant detected a total of 446 metabolites (after removal of the data with CV_QC > 20%)
